title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government's response outlines how they will address the recommendations made by a committee to establish standards for the development and use of artificial intelligence in public services. The response aims to ensure artificial intelligence is used responsibly and in a way that benefits the public.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This report assesses the impact of using copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence systems, examining how existing copyright laws apply to AI. It aims to provide insight into the rights and implications of utilizing copyrighted materials in AI development.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The UK Department for Education has issued a policy on the use of generative artificial intelligence in education. This policy outlines the department's stance on generative AI in the education sector.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport artificial intelligence action plan outlines a strategy for the Department for Transport (DfT) to collaborate with the transport sector, aiming to maximize the benefits of AI while addressing potential associated risks. This plan will facilitate the safe and efficient integration of AI technologies across various transportation modes.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This report documents the progress of using copyright-protected works to develop artificial intelligence systems under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act. It likely provides updates on the use of copyrighted materials in AI development.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is responding to recommendations from the Regulatory Horizons Council to better regulate AI used in medical settings. The response aims to improve safety and oversight of AI as a medical device.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: Ofqual's policy document outlines their approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications. The document aims to provide guidance on the use of AI in the qualifications sector, in response to government requests for clarity.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to a report on artificial intelligence, stating its intentions to address concerns and create regulatory frameworks to manage AI. The response aims to ensure the UK's AI development supports public trust and safety.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference for a committee's review into artificial intelligence (AI) and public standards. The review is aimed at developing guidelines or standards related to AI and its impact on the public.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The CSPL has submitted evidence to the Science and Technology Committee about the governance of Artificial Intelligence. The submission addresses the current state of AI governance, likely providing recommendations for improvement.
